research_basis: Literacy development and written communication, social communication and etiquette, graphic design principles, typography, cardmaking traditions, digital and traditional communication, social-emotional learning through written expression, cultural invitation customs

metadata.research_alignment: Literacy development including emergent writing, written communication conventions and etiquette, social-emotional learning through written expression, graphic design principles including typography and color theory, cardmaking and paper crafting traditions across cultures, digital and traditional invitation methods, communication psychology and effectiveness research, cultural protocol and inclusive design practices, mathematical and computational models for design and distribution systems

coverage_details.kids_4_under: Sensory vocabulary for cardmaking materials, basic tools and supplies, simple decoration techniques, concept of mailing and postal delivery, foundation for understanding written communication, recognition of cards and their purpose
coverage_details.kids_10_under: Historical cardmaking traditions, design principles like symmetry and color, typography basics, formal vs casual invitation formats, understanding audience and written tone, literacy skills in creating messages, introduction to graphic design concepts
coverage_details.teenagers: Cognitive and neurological development in written communication, design software and digital tools, social-emotional dimensions of invitations, typography hierarchy and kerning, color psychology and visual perception, social media invitation sharing, audience awareness and register adjustment, contemporary design movements
coverage_details.adults: Historical and cultural contexts of invitations and cards, social theory perspectives on written communication as social performance, design movements from Art Deco to contemporary minimalism, branding and personal identity through invitations, business communication protocols, environmental and inclusive design ethics, psychological research on handwritten vs digital effectiveness
coverage_details.no_humanity: Graph algorithms for invitation distribution networks, machine learning for design and attendance prediction, information theory and entropy in communication, control systems for printing optimization, game theory of social responses, topological and geometric analysis of card structures, category theory for design abstraction, symbolic computation for variation generation
